air: noun. The substance constituting Earth 's atmosphere: a gaseous mixture of nitrogen, oxygen, and various trace gases.
aphid: noun. A sap - sucking insect pest of the superfamily Aphidoidea; an aphidian.
apish: adjective. Resembling or characteristic of an ape.
ash: noun. Solid remains of a fire.
dah: noun. The spoken representation of a dash in radio and telegraph Morse code.
dais: noun. A raised platform in a room for a high table, a seat of honour, a throne, or other dignified occupancy, such as ancestral statues; a
dash: noun. Any of the following symbols: ‒ (figure dash), – (en dash),, (em dash), or ― (horizontal bar).
dashi: noun. A type of soup or cooking stock, often made from kelp.
dip: noun. A lower section of a road or geological feature.
dish: noun. A vessel such as a plate for holding or serving food, often flat with a depressed region in the middle.
drip: verb. To fall one drop at a time.
had: verb. simple past and past participle of have
hair: noun. A pigmented filament of keratin which grows from a follicle on the skin of humans and other mammals.
hap: noun. A person's lot (good or bad), luck, fortune, fate.
hardship: noun. Difficulty or trouble; hard times.
harp: noun. A musical instrument consisting of a body and a curved neck, strung with strings of varying length that are stroked or plucked with
harsh: adjective. Unpleasantly rough to the touch or other senses.
has: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of have
hash: noun. Food, especially meat and potatoes, chopped and mixed together. corn-beef hash
hasp: noun. A clasp, especially a metal strap fastened by a padlock or a pin; also, a hook for fastening a door.
hip: noun. The outward-projecting parts of the pelvis and top of the femur and the overlying tissue.
pad: noun. A flattened mass of anything soft, to sit or lie on.
padi: noun. Alternative form of paddy (“type of rice”).
pah: interjection. Used to express distaste, disgust or outrage.
paid: verb. simple past and past participle of pay
pair: noun. Two alike or identical things taken together; often followed by of.
parish: noun. An administrative part of a diocese that has its own church.
pas: noun. The right of going foremost; precedence.
pash: verb. To snog, to make out, to kiss.
phi: noun. Φ, the 21st letter of the Euclidean and modern Greek alphabet, usually romanized as "ph".
pia: noun. The pia mater, the innermost of the meninges that protect the brain and spinal cord.
psi: noun. The twenty-third letter of Classical and Modern Greek and the twenty-fifth letter of Old and Ancient Greek.
radish: noun. A plant of the Brassicaceae family, Raphanus sativus or Raphanus raphanistrum subsp. sativus, having an edible root.
rah: interjection. An exclamation of support or encouragement.
raid: noun. A quick hostile or predatory incursion or invasion in a battle.
rap: noun. A sharp blow with something hard.
ras: noun. An Ethiopian king or prince.
rash: adjective. Acting too quickly without considering the consequences and risks; not careful; hasty.
rasp: noun. A coarse file or filelike tool, on which the cutting prominences are distinct points raised by the oblique stroke of a sharp punch,
ria: noun. A narrow inlet of the sea formed by a flooded unglaciated river valley.
rid: verb. Followed by of: to free (oneself or someone, or a place) from an annoyance or hindrance.
rids: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of rid
rip: verb. To divide or separate the parts of (especially something flimsy, such as paper or fabric), by cutting or tearing; to tear off or out
said: verb. simple past and past participle of say
sap: noun. The juice of plants of any kind, especially the ascending and descending juices or circulating fluid essential to nutrition.
sard: noun. A variety of carnelian, of a rich reddish yellow or brownish red color.
sari: noun. The traditional dress of women in the Indian Subcontinent; an outer garment consisting of a single length of cotton or silk, most
shad: noun. Any one of several species of food fishes that make up the genus Alosa in the family Clupeidae, to which the herrings also belong;
shah: noun. A king of Persia or Iran.
shard: noun. A piece of broken glass or pottery, especially one found in an archaeological dig.
sharp: adjective. Terminating in a point or edge, especially one that can cut or pierce easily; not dull, obtuse, or rounded.
ship: noun. A water - borne vessel generally larger than a boat.
sip: noun. A small mouthful of drink.
sir: noun. A man of a higher rank or position.
spa: noun. A health resort near a mineral spring or hot spring.
spahi: noun. An Ottoman (Turkish empire) cavalryman, especially as recruited under a land-based system.
spar: noun. A rafter of a roof.
sparid: noun. Any of several perciform fishes of the family Sparidae
sri: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Siriano.
adh: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Adhola.
aph: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Athpare.
ard: noun. A simple plough consisting of a spike dragged through the soil.
dar: noun. A fish found in the Severn River; a dart or dace.
dha: noun. Alternative form of dah (“Burmese knife”).
ida: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Idakho-Isukha-Tiriki.
pahi: noun. A large war canoe of Tahiti.
pais: noun. The country (ie: the jury); also, the people living in the district from where the jury is taken.
phd: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Phudagi.
pid: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Piaroa.
pir: noun. A Muslim holy man or religious leader, especially Sufi.
psia: noun. Initialism of pounds per square inch absolute.
rais: noun. Alternative form of reis (“holder of military rank”).
risp: verb. To rub together, to rasp or grate.
sai: noun. A handheld weapon with three prongs, used in some Oriental martial arts.
shahi: noun. A former currency unit of Iran, worth one twentieth of a qiran.
shp: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Shipibo-Conibo.
shrap: noun. A place baited with chaff to entice birds.
sida: noun. any of the flowering plants of the genus Sida in the mallow family
spad: noun. A government adviser, often in a political or media role.
spraid: adjective. Alternative form of sprayed (“chapped with cold”).
